player_flags.name: Casemiro
player_flags.news: Confirmed NOT in squad for GW38. Man United officially confirmed Casemiro departs at end of 2025-26 season. His farewell appearance was the previous gameweek. Michael Carrick confirmed he will not feature vs Brighton.

player_flags.name: Haaland
player_flags.news: Left out of Man City's final day clash with Aston Villa. Guardiola rested Haaland, Rodri, Donnarumma, O'Reilly and Guehi as part of heavy rotation in his farewell game. Haaland won the Golden Boot (27 goals) but did not play GW38.

player_flags.name: O'Reilly
player_flags.news: Rested for Man City GW38 vs Aston Villa. Explicitly named among the players given the afternoon off by Guardiola alongside Haaland, Rodri, Donnarumma and Guehi.

player_flags.name: Saka
player_flags.news: High rotation risk for Arsenal's final day vs Crystal Palace. Saka trained individually on Thursday managing a knock. Arsenal are champions and Arteta was expected to rest key players with a Champions League final vs PSG imminent. Multiple sources list Saka as a rest candidate. Confirmed missing or bench in most predicted XIs.

player_flags.name: Rice
player_flags.news: Rotation risk for Arsenal GW38 vs Crystal Palace. Arsenal are champions with UCL final imminent. Rice listed among substitutes in predicted lineups across multiple FPL outlets. However, a thehardtackle.com source indicates Rice was expected to return to the XI vs PSG after being rested in GW38, confirming the rest happened.

player_flags.name: Gabriel
player_flags.news: Moderate rotation risk. Arsenal champions and rotating for UCL final prep. Crystal Palace also rotated heavily for their Conference League final. Some sources suggest Gabriel could still lead the backline; others expect young replacements. Uncertain.

player_flags.name: Canvot
player_flags.news: Crystal Palace rotated heavily in GW38 to protect players for the UEFA Conference League Final vs Rayo Vallecano (which Palace won 1-0 on June 1). Canvot is a bench asset and at high risk of not starting given Palace's full squad rotation ahead of the European final.

player_flags.name: B.Fernandes
player_flags.news: Confirmed starter and outstanding. Bruno Fernandes played 90 minutes in Man United's 3-0 win at Brighton (GW38), scoring 1 goal and breaking the Premier League assists record.

player_flags.name: Bowen
player_flags.news: Confirmed key starter. West Ham in relegation fight needing a win vs Leeds to survive. Bowen is their talisman, confirmed on penalties, with 2 double-digit hauls in last 3 home games. 134,000+ FPL transfers in for GW38. Top motivation confirmed. Captain-grade.

player_flags.name: Thiago
player_flags.news: Expected to start for Brentford vs Nottingham Forest. No injury flag. Golden Boot runner-up with 22 goals. No rotation signals from Thomas Frank. Clean availability status in features.json.

player_flags.name: Anderson
player_flags.news: Top GW38 transfer target with 90,000+ new owners. In-form — 5 goals and 2 assists in last 4 matches. Expected starter for Nottingham Forest vs Brentford. No injury news. Nailed.

player_flags.name: Thiaw
player_flags.news: Confirmed in Newcastle's starting XI vs Chelsea for GW38. No injury news. Newcastle have European qualification motivation. Nailed starter.

player_flags.name: Hill
player_flags.news: No injury news. Clean availability in features.json. Bournemouth have no major end-of-season stakes. No explicit GW38 lineup confirmation found but no concerns flagged anywhere.

player_flags.name: Verbruggen
player_flags.news: Confirmed first-choice Brighton GK. Brighton hosted Man United in GW38 (Brighton lost 0-3 per confirmed result). No rotation risk for a GK. Named in predicted GW38 XI. Dutch first choice heading into 2026 World Cup.

player_flags.name: Dubravka
player_flags.news: Bench GK only. No injury flag. Burnley are relegated — squad rotation possible at end-of-season fixture but Dubravka is a bench asset with minimal fantasy impact regardless.

key_alerts.alert: CRITICAL — Confirmed absent for GW38 vs Aston Villa. Guardiola rested Haaland explicitly in his farewell game. A 14.7m starting asset producing zero points is a severe blow. Immediate contingency required: likely auto-sub from bench but bench options at MCI (O'Reilly) also rested.
CONFIRMED OUT — Will not feature in GW38 vs Brighton at all. Farewell was the previous gameweek, departure from club confirmed at season end. Dead bench asset. Zero points guaranteed.

HIGH ROTATION RISK — Arsenal are champions with a UCL final vs PSG imminent. Saka trained individually with a knock and is a leading rest candidate. Rice predicted on the bench in multiple outlets. Both are starting picks with combined cost of 17.2m. If neither starts, significant points lost from the XI.
